trivia_bullets.text: The Single Strike and Rapid Strike mechanics were introduced in Battle Styles (SWSH5, March 2021) and continued through Chilling Reign (SWSH6, June 2021). Single Strike cards represent hard-hitting, all-or-nothing combat philosophy (contrast: Rapid Strike = technical, multi-hit). The mechanic is tied narratively to Kubfu/Urshifu's two forms — Single Strike Style (Dark-type) and Rapid Strike Style (Water-type). [pokemon.com: 'Rapid Strike Style Mustard, Tower of Darkness' article; pokebeach.com: Battle Styles introduction]
sources: pokebeach.com: Battle Styles introduction article, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Chilling Reign no. 154/198, Uncommon Pokemon Tool, illustrated by 5ban Graphics. No flavor text. 5ban Graphics is a major studio illustrator responsible for a large share of SWSH-era V/VMAX cards and Item cards; their signature is clean, high-contrast graphic design — well suited to the stylized scroll aesthetic here. The Scroll's Bullet Breakthrough attack (120 damage, unaffected by Weakness/Resistance and opponent effects) is designed as a tech option grafted onto a Single Strike Pokemon via the Tool mechanic rather than a standalone attack. [pokemon.com TCG database: SWSH6 154]

The Single Strike Scroll cycle in the corpus now has three confirmed members: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn (Battle Styles, no. 133/163) — trivia-enriched; Urn of Vitality (Battle Styles, no. 139/163) — trivia-enriched; Single Strike Scroll of Piercing (Chilling Reign, no. 154/198) — this card. Scroll of Scorn deals damage based on your own damage counters; Scroll of Piercing bypasses all modifiers flat. Tower of Darkness (Battle Styles stadium) is the cycle's draw engine. Houndoom's ability accelerates Single Strike Energy for the attack costs. [pokemon.com TCG database; Battle Styles corpus trivia; codedyellow.com: Pokemon Single Strike Card List, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: codedyellow.com: Pokemon Single Strike Card List, via WebSearch snippet
